content.text: The PO-33 has minimal but versatile connectivity: two 3.5mm jacks on the top edge (line input and line output/sync), a built-in microphone, and a built-in speaker. Understanding how these work together is key to getting the most from the device.

Both 3.5mm ports are located on the TOP EDGE of the PO-33, facing the back of the unit. The line input is on the left side, and the line output/sync port is on the right side. This placement keeps cables out of the way during operation.

content.caption: Both ports are on the top edge, facing backward

content.text: The right-side port (marked OUT) serves double duty as both the audio output and sync I/O. The left channel carries sync clock, the right channel carries mono audio.

items: Use stereo headphones directly for monitoring
Connect to speakers or mixer using a stereo cable
Use a Y-splitter to separate sync and audio
Chain to other Pocket Operators for synchronized jamming

content.caption: Line output / sync port on top-right edge

content.title: Headphone Output
content.text: When in SY0 mode (sync off), the output port sends stereo audio through both channels for normal headphone use.
The left-side port (marked LINE IN) accepts mono audio from external sources for sampling. When a cable is connected, it automatically disables the built-in microphone.

items: 3.5mm mono or stereo input (left channel used if stereo)
Line-level signals from phones, computers, synths
No gain control - use source volume to adjust level
Provides cleaner recordings than built-in mic

content.caption: Line input port on top-left edge

content.text: The red microphone icon in the top-right corner of the front panel indicates the built-in electret microphone. It's always available for sampling when no line input is connected.
The small onboard speaker provides mono audio output for quick monitoring without external gear. It's automatically muted when headphones or other devices are connected to the output port.

content.title: Speaker Limitations
content.text: The built-in speaker is useful for portability but lacks bass response and volume. Use headphones or external speakers for serious production.
To record the PO-33's output to a computer, phone, or other recording device:

items: Set sync mode to SY0 (for stereo output) or SY1 (if you want sync clock on left channel)
Connect output port to recording device's line input
Adjust input gain on the recording device
Press play and record your performance

rows: First SY1, middle SY3, last SY2
